app.title: Compatibilité MBTI & Conseils
app.description: Découvre la compatibilité des types MBTI, l'analyse de personnalité et les conseils de rencontres/travail. Qui est ta correspondance parfaite?

header.title: Compatibilité MBTI & Conseils de Vie
header.subtitle: Sélectionnez votre type et découvrez vos meilleures correspondances

share.copied: Résultat copié! Partagez avec vos amis 🎉
share.compareTitle: Je suis {type}! Quelle est votre compatibilité MBTI?
share.bestDesc: {type1} et {type2} se complètent parfaitement. Vous pouvez maximiser vos forces respectives et combler les lacunes.
share.goodDesc: {type1} et {type2} travaillent bien ensemble. Avec un peu d'effort, vous pouvez construire une relation plus profonde.
share.badDesc: {type1} et {type2} peuvent avoir des différences de personnalité, mais se comprendre peut être une opportunité de croissance.
share.neutralDesc: {type1} et {type2} n'ont pas de forces ou de faiblesses particulières. Mais avec de l'effort, cela peut se développer en une bonne relation.

error.storage: Certaines fonctions peuvent être limitées en mode privé.
error.data: Erreur de chargement. Veuillez rafraîchir la page.
error.canvas: Problème de rendu graphique. Veuillez réessayer.
error.general: Un problème est survenu. Rafraîchissez ou réessayez.

INTJ.title: Le Stratège
INTJ.description: Imaginative and strategic thinker with a plan for everything. Independent and determined, moving toward goals with high standards and a dislike for inefficiency.

love.0: Even if expressing feelings is hard, try telling your partner you care
love.1: Don't just analyze your partner's emotional needs logically

work.0: Be patient when sharing ideas with team members
work.1: Practice explaining your vision in simple terms
work.2: Don't try to do everything alone; consider delegating
work.3: Participate in team building even if it seems inefficient

friend.0: Value your friends' everyday conversations too
friend.1: Sometimes just listening is better than giving advice
friend.2: Sometimes they just want emotional support
friend.3: Focusing on a few deep relationships is also great

INTJ.psychology: INTJs have a rich inner world of imagination. They likely asked 'why?' a lot as children and excel at understanding systems and patterns. They prioritize logic over emotions, but this doesn't mean they lack feelings. They feel deeply but may not know how to express it.
INTJ.growth: Focus on developing emotional intelligence (EQ). Let go of perfectionism a little and accept failure as a learning opportunity. Acknowledging that others' methods can also be valuable will improve your relationships.
INTJ.career: Strategy consultant, scientist, software architect, investment analyst, writer — jobs that allow independent deep thinking are ideal.
INTJ.stress: Under stress, you may become unusually impulsive or obsess over details. Alone time and meditation help with recovery.

INTP.title: Le Logicien
INTP.description: An innovative inventor with an endless thirst for knowledge. Loves logic and theory, enjoys exploring abstract ideas. Quiet but always generating new ideas internally.

love.0: Practice expressing interest through words and actions
love.1: Pay more attention to your partner's emotional needs
love.2: Learn to distinguish when empathy is needed over analysis

work.0: Turning ideas into action is also important
work.1: Create a system to meet deadlines
work.2: Share your insights in team meetings
work.3: Even small tasks are part of the big picture

friend.0: Pay more attention to your friends' emotions
friend.1: Sometimes just being there is enough
friend.2: Not every conversation needs to be a debate
friend.3: Set reminders so you don't forget plans

INTP.psychology: INTPs are true thinkers. They gain energy from understanding complex systems and exploring new ideas. They tend to question social norms and constantly ask 'why should it be that way?'
INTP.growth: You need practice turning ideas into reality. A 'good enough' execution can be more valuable than a perfect theory. Try feeling emotions instead of trying to understand them logically.
INTP.career: Researcher, philosopher, programmer, mathematician, analyst — jobs that involve independent thinking and problem-solving are ideal.
INTP.stress: Under stress, you may worry excessively or become unusually emotionally sensitive. Simple activities like walks or puzzles can help.

ENTJ.title: Le Commandant
ENTJ.description: A bold, imaginative, strong-willed leader who always finds or creates a way. Values efficiency and results, excels at leading others toward goals.

love.0: Don't treat your partner like a 'project'
love.1: Sometimes harmony matters more than winning
love.3: Showing vulnerability is also a form of strength

work.0: Take time to listen to team members' opinions
work.2: Be constructive with criticism, generous with praise

friend.0: Don't always try to lead every conversation
friend.1: Celebrate your friends' small achievements too
friend.2: Sometimes support is needed more than advice
friend.3: Keep your promises — trust is important

ENTJ.psychology: ENTJs are born leaders. They likely led groups or planned activities from childhood. They have little patience for inefficiency and prefer fast decision-making.
ENTJ.growth: Developing emotional intelligence will elevate your leadership. Understanding that process matters as much as results, and that people don't move on logic alone, will give you greater influence.
ENTJ.career: CEO, management consultant, lawyer, judge, entrepreneur — jobs where you can exercise leadership are ideal.
ENTJ.stress: Under stress, you may become more aggressive or try to control even more. Talking with someone you trust helps.

ENTP.title: Le Débatteur
ENTP.description: Smart, curious thinker who enjoys intellectual challenges. Loves debate and isn't afraid to challenge conventions. Lives in a world of ideas.

love.0: Don't turn everything into a debate
love.1: Don't tell your partner their feelings are 'wrong'
love.2: Deeply focusing on one relationship has value too
love.3: Follow through on what you say

work.1: Consider your team members' feelings too

friend.0: Don't just refute your friends' opinions
friend.1: Sometimes just agreeing is fine too
friend.2: Deep, lasting friendships are valuable too
friend.3: Hold back on jokes when a friend is feeling down

ENTP.psychology: ENTPs are alchemists of ideas. They gain energy from exploring new possibilities and challenging existing systems. They enjoy playing 'devil's advocate' and sometimes treat debate like a sport.
ENTP.growth: You need practice finishing what you start. You're easily captivated by new ideas, but building execution skills can lead to truly great results. Don't dismiss others' emotions as 'irrational.'
ENTP.career: Entrepreneur, lawyer, marketer, inventor, comedian — jobs where you can think creatively and explore new ideas are ideal.
ENTP.stress: Under stress, you may become more argumentative or obsess over trivial things. New environments or travel can help refresh you.

INFJ.title: L'Avocat
INFJ.description: Quiet, mysterious, and inspiring idealist. Has a deep desire to help others and make the world a better place. One of the rarest personality types.

love.0: Don't try to solve everything alone
love.1: Express your own needs to your partner too
love.2: Accept that no relationship is perfect
love.3: Try to understand rather than change your partner

work.1: You can't help everyone — set boundaries
work.2: Don't just avoid conflict; resolve it constructively
work.3: Let go of perfectionism a little

friend.0: Don't let friends take advantage of you
friend.1: Remember that your needs matter too
friend.2: You don't need to solve every friend's problems
friend.3: Speak up when you need alone time

INFJ.psychology: INFJs have deep empathy and intuition. They can read others' emotions almost telepathically. But this ability can sometimes be a burden, as they may absorb others' emotions and become exhausted.
INFJ.growth: Setting boundaries for yourself is crucial. Helping others is great, but you need to fill your own cup first. Forgive yourself when you can't reach your perfect vision.
INFJ.career: Counselor, psychologist, writer, teacher, social worker — jobs where you can help people and create meaningful change are ideal.
INFJ.stress: Under extreme stress, you might suddenly cut off relationships in what's known as a 'door slam.' Regular self-care and talking with someone you trust are important.

INFP.title: Le Médiateur
INFP.description: Poetic, kind, and altruistic, always ready to help for a good cause. Follows deep inner values and ideals, and values authenticity highly.

love.0: Love your real partner, not an idealized version
love.1: Don't avoid conflict — handle it in a healthy way
love.3: Don't expect your partner to read your mind

work.1: Create a system to meet deadlines
work.2: Work that doesn't align with your values is sometimes necessary
work.3: Apply your creativity in practical ways

friend.0: Share everyday things with your friends too
friend.1: Don't only seek deep conversations; enjoy light ones too
friend.2: Don't be too hurt by friends' small mistakes
friend.3: Taking the initiative to reach out is good too

INFP.psychology: INFPs have a deep inner world. They hold strong values and ideals, pursuing authenticity and meaning. They have excellent artistic sensitivity and often express themselves through writing or art.
INFP.growth: Finding balance between ideals and reality is important. Dream of a perfect world, but also acknowledge the value of small changes. Practice not taking criticism as a personal attack.
INFP.career: Writer, counselor, artist, graphic designer, social worker — jobs where you can express creativity and values are ideal.
INFP.stress: Under stress, you may escape from reality or fall into self-criticism. Creative activities or time in nature help with recovery.

ENFJ.title: Le Protagoniste
ENFJ.description: Charismatic and inspiring leader who can captivate an audience. Finds great joy in helping others grow and naturally leads people.

love.0: Don't be overly controlling in the name of 'helping'
love.1: Prioritizing your own needs isn't selfish
love.3: It's okay not to maintain harmony at all times

work.0: Validate yourself without needing others' approval
work.3: Watch out for burnout — self-care matters

friend.0: It's okay to let friends solve things on their own
friend.1: You don't need to be the 'savior' in every relationship
friend.2: Share your own struggles with friends too
friend.3: Allow yourself to receive in relationships

ENFJ.psychology: ENFJs excel at seeing others' potential and drawing it out. They are natural mentors and coaches. But they tend to exhaust themselves helping others.
ENFJ.growth: Learn that prioritizing your own needs isn't selfish. Understand that you can't save everyone and that people need the opportunity to learn on their own.
ENFJ.career: Teacher, counselor, HR professional, coach, politician — jobs where you can lead and develop people are ideal.
ENFJ.stress: Under stress, you may become self-critical or overly involved in others' problems. Healthy boundary-setting is important.

ENFP.title: L'Activiste
ENFP.description: Enthusiastic, creative, and sociable free spirit. Always discovering new possibilities and inspiring others with passion.

love.0: Your quest for novelty can make your partner anxious
love.1: Keep your promises — trust is important
love.2: Value the everyday aspects of your relationship too
love.3: Learn to deeply focus on one relationship

friend.0: Deep friendships are just as valuable as many friends
friend.1: Listen to your friends' stories all the way through
friend.3: Sometimes quiet time with friends is needed too

ENFP.psychology: ENFPs are explorers of possibility. They are endlessly drawn to new ideas and experiences. They crave deep emotional connections and pursue authentic relationships. They start passionately but may struggle to maintain things when interest wanes.
ENFP.growth: Practice finishing what you start. Enthusiasm for new things is a strength, but deep expertise is also valuable. Daily routines can actually be a foundation for freedom.
ENFP.career: Marketer, writer, actor, counselor, entrepreneur — jobs where you can be creative and connect with people are ideal.
ENFP.stress: Under stress, you may become excessively self-critical or obsess over trivial things. New environments or creative activities help with recovery.

ISTJ.title: Le Réaliste
ISTJ.description: Fact-based thinker with a strong sense of responsibility. Always keeps promises and values tradition and order. Known as someone you can rely on.

love.0: Practice expressing emotions — it matters to your partner
love.1: Be a little more open to change
love.2: Sometimes enjoy things without a plan

work.1: Not everything needs to be perfect
work.2: Team members' creative ideas have value too

friend.0: Listen to your friends' emotional needs too
friend.1: Sometimes listening is better than offering solutions

ISTJ.psychology: ISTJs seek stability and order. They keep promises faithfully and have a strong sense of responsibility. They prefer tradition and proven methods, valuing stability over change. They have rich inner emotions but may find them hard to express.
ISTJ.growth: Learn that change can be an opportunity, not a threat. Understanding that expressing emotions isn't weakness will deepen your relationships. Sometimes 'good enough' is okay.
ISTJ.career: Accountant, lawyer, military officer, project manager, inspector — systematic and responsible roles are ideal.
ISTJ.stress: Under stress, you may become more rigid or fixate on worst-case scenarios. Reliable routines and support from close ones help.

ISFJ.title: Le Défenseur
ISFJ.description: Very devoted and warm protector, always ready to defend loved ones. Quiet but social, seeking stability and harmony.

love.0: Remember that your needs matter too
love.1: You don't need to do everything your partner doesn't ask for
love.2: Don't avoid conflict — handle it healthily
love.3: Don't hold on to past mistakes for too long

work.0: Don't overwork just to be recognized
work.2: It's important to show your contributions

friend.0: Don't let friends take advantage of you
friend.1: It's okay to ask for help too

ISFJ.psychology: ISFJs are quiet heroes. They find deep satisfaction in caring for others, often putting their own needs last. They pay attention to details and cherish lessons learned from past experiences.
ISFJ.growth: Learn that putting your own needs first isn't selfish. Saying 'no' to others is also a healthy boundary. Change isn't always bad.
ISFJ.career: Nurse, teacher, social worker, administrator, librarian — jobs where you can care for others and maintain order are ideal.
ISFJ.stress: Under stress, you may hide yourself more rather than asserting, or imagine worst-case scenarios. Opening up to someone you trust helps.

ESTJ.title: Le Dirigeant
ESTJ.description: Excellent manager who excels at managing people and things. Honest, devoted, values tradition and order. Leads organizations with strong leadership.

love.0: Your partner's feelings are as important as data
love.1: You don't always need to be right — learn to compromise
love.2: Softness is also a part of strength

friend.0: Don't ignore your friends' emotional needs
friend.1: Sometimes just listening is better than giving advice

ESTJ.psychology: ESTJs are born organizers. They like clear structures and rules and dislike disorder. They are direct and honest, acting as they speak. Sometimes they may appear inflexible.
ESTJ.growth: Learn that emotions are as important as facts. Not every situation can have rules applied. Recognizing that others' methods can be valuable will make your leadership more effective.
ESTJ.career: Manager, executive, judge, military officer, financial officer — jobs where you can lead and manage organizations are ideal.
ESTJ.stress: Under stress, you may become more controlling or critical. Exercise or activities that give a sense of achievement help.

ESFJ.title: Le Consul
ESFJ.description: Very caring, social, and popular. Loves helping others and strives to create a harmonious environment. Plays a central role in the community.

love.0: You don't need to sacrifice everything for your partner
love.1: Conflict is normal — don't avoid it
love.2: Don't worry too much about others' opinions
love.3: It's okay that your partner is different from you

work.0: You don't need to meet everyone's expectations
work.2: Your opinion matters too — speak up

friend.0: You don't need to solve all your friends' problems
friend.1: Don't spend too much energy on gossip
friend.2: Accept friends who are different from you

ESFJ.psychology: ESFJs are guardians of social harmony. They are sensitive to others' needs and work to make everyone feel comfortable. They value tradition and customs and often play central roles in communities.
ESFJ.growth: Learn to value yourself without others' approval. Conflict isn't always bad. Treat your own needs as important as others'.
ESFJ.career: Nurse, teacher, event planner, HR representative, sales — jobs where you can be with people and care for them are ideal.
ESFJ.stress: Under stress, you may feel deeply hurt if you feel unappreciated. Support and expressions of gratitude from close ones help.

ISTP.title: L'Artisan
ISTP.description: Bold and practical experimenter. Loves building and taking things apart, stays calm in crises. Values freedom highly.

love.1: Share your thoughts with your partner
love.2: Acknowledge that emotional needs are important too

work.0: Share your insights in team meetings
work.3: Try to understand why rules exist

friend.0: Join your friends' emotional conversations sometimes
friend.2: Reach out to friends first sometimes

ISTP.psychology: ISTPs are practical problem solvers. They love building, analyzing, and fixing things. They are remarkably calm in crises and respond logically. They highly value personal freedom and independence.
ISTP.growth: Acknowledge that emotions are an important source of information. Maintaining long-term commitments deepens relationships. Paying more attention to others' emotional needs will improve your relationships.
ISTP.career: Engineer, technician, pilot, paramedic, athlete — jobs using practical skills with autonomy are ideal.
ISTP.stress: Under stress, you may isolate further or engage in reckless behavior. Hands-on activities or time in nature help.

ISFP.title: L'Aventurier
ISFP.description: Flexible and charming artist, ready to explore and try new experiences. Quiet on the outside but burning with inner passion. Lives in the present moment.

love.1: Don't avoid conflict — talk it through
love.3: Don't take your partner's criticism as a personal attack

work.0: Create a system to meet deadlines

friend.0: Reach out to friends first sometimes

ISFP.psychology: ISFPs are quiet artists. They pursue beauty and harmony, enjoying sensory experiences. They have strong inner values but may struggle to express them. They value living in the present moment.
ISFP.growth: Practice expressing your emotions and values in words. Handling conflict healthily rather than avoiding it will deepen your relationships. Future planning is sometimes necessary.
ISFP.career: Artist, designer, musician, chef, nurse — jobs that utilize emotion and aesthetic sense are ideal.
ISFP.stress: Under stress, you may isolate further or fall into self-criticism. Artistic activities or time in nature help with recovery.

ESTP.title: L'Entrepreneur
ESTP.description: Smart, energetic, and enjoys taking risks. Quickly assesses situations and acts immediately. Loves adventure and thrills.

love.0: Pay attention to your partner's emotional needs too

work.2: Consider your team members' feelings too

friend.2: Not everything needs to be spontaneous

ESTP.psychology: ESTPs are people of action. They are fully immersed in the present moment, with quick judgment and adaptability. They enjoy thrills and adventure and dislike boredom. Their charisma naturally draws people to follow them.
ESTP.growth: Practice considering long-term consequences. Others' emotions are also important information. Sometimes stopping to think is also necessary.
ESTP.career: Entrepreneur, sales professional, athlete, paramedic, marketer — jobs requiring quick judgment and action are ideal.
ESTP.stress: Under stress, you may become more impulsive or engage in risky behavior. Physical activity or new experiences help with stress relief.

ESFP.title: L'Amuseur
ESFP.description: Spontaneous, energetic, and passionate entertainer. Life is a party and you're the star. Loves enjoying the moment and making people happy.

work.0: Pay attention to deadlines and details
work.1: Not every task will be fun

friend.0: Respect your friends' quiet needs too
friend.2: A party atmosphere isn't always needed

ESFP.psychology: ESFPs are people who enjoy life's celebrations. They are fully immersed in the present, love bringing joy to people, enjoy sensory experiences, and are spontaneous and flexible. They enjoy being the center of attention.
ESFP.growth: Practice considering long-term consequences. Life has responsibilities as well as parties. Serious conversations and deep relationships are also valuable. Planning for the future is sometimes necessary.
ESFP.career: Entertainer, event planner, sales, tour guide, teacher — jobs where you can be with people and bring joy are ideal.
ESFP.stress: Under stress, you may escape from reality or seek excessive stimulation. Time with close friends or creative activities help.

about.text1: Sélectionnez votre type MBTI pour voir votre profil et analyse de compatibilité.
about.heading2: Tirer le meilleur des conseils MBTI
about.text2: Comprendre les forces et faiblesses de chaque type aide à construire de meilleures relations.
